meaning: adj.盲目的爱国心的,沙文主义的
examples: My ex-boyfriend Anthony was very chauvinistic.
The student who wrote "The A&P as a State of Mind" wisely dropped a paragraph that questioned whether Sammy displays chauvinistic attitudes toward women.
The tribe has been forced to modify its chauvinistic attitudes.
Italian society remains deeply chauvinistic, according to her, and television is the strongest tool for change.
If you are bold or chauvinistic, you could bet on a further appreciation of the dollar against the euro.
